Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents an item available for sale in the Bounsi Bazaar store.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

category.description: The category the product belongs to (e.g., Food, Electronic, Fashion).

imageUrl.description: The URL of the product's image.

description.description: A detailed description of the product, potentially generated by an AI tool.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the product was first added to the store.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the product's information was last updated.

StoreSetting.title: StoreSetting
StoreSetting.description: Stores global configuration and aggregated statistics for the Bounsi Bazaar application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StoreSetting entity. This should typically be a fixed value like 'app-settings' as it represents a singleton.

totalOrders.description: The cumulative count of all orders recorded across the store.

totalEarnings.description: The cumulative revenue generated from all recorded orders.

activeThemeName.description: The name of the currently active festival theme (e.g., 'Diwali', 'Christmas', 'Normal').

lastUpdated.description: Timestamp of the last time these store settings or statistics were updated.

definition.description: Stores all products available for sale in the Bounsi Bazaar. Documents in this collection are publicly readable by all users for catalog display and search. Only authenticated administrators are authorized to create, update, or delete product documents. This supports robust QAPs for list operations.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific product.

definition.description: A marker collection used for Database Access Control (DBAC). The existence of a document at a path `/admin_roles/{adminUid}` signifies that the user with UID `{adminUid}` has administrative privileges. These documents are typically simple, containing minimal data like an 'assignedAt' timestamp.

params.name: adminUid
params.description: The unique user ID (UID) of an administrator.

definition.description: A singleton document holding all global store configurations and aggregated statistics (e.e.g., totalOrders, totalEarnings, activeThemeName). Access to this document (read/write) is restricted solely to administrators. The 'activeThemeName' field is denormalized to '/publicDisplaySettings/theme' for public read access.

A singleton document containing only the 'activeThemeName' field, denormalized from '/storeSettings/mainSettings'. This document is publicly readable by all users to display the active festive theme without exposing sensitive administrative data or requiring complex security rules. This ensures Authorization Independence for public theme access.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for the 'Bounsi Bazaar' application is designed with a strong emphasis on Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and adherence to QAPs (Queries Are Not Filters). 

1.  **Authorization Independence & QAPs for Products:**
    *   Products are stored in a top-level collection `/products/{productId}`. All documents in this collection share a homogeneous security posture: they are publicly readable by any user (authenticated or unauthenticated) and writable/deletable only by administrators. 
    *   This design directly supports QAPs because `list` operations on `/products` do not require any security rule filtering; all documents are intentionally made public. This makes product catalog display and search functionality highly efficient and easy to secure with simple `allow read: true;` rules for this path. 

2.  **Authorization Independence & QAPs for Store Settings and Public Display:**
    *   The `StoreSetting` entity, which originally contained both admin-sensitive statistics (`totalOrders`, `totalEarnings`) and publicly visible data (`activeThemeName`), posed a challenge for Authorization Independence and Structural Segregation.
    *   **Denormalization Strategy:** To address this, the `StoreSetting` entity has been split conceptually and denormalized across two paths:
        *   `/storeSettings/mainSettings`: This singleton document holds all `StoreSetting` fields, including sensitive statistics. It has an admin-only security posture (read and write). This ensures that administrative data is strictly protected, fulfilling DBAC principles.
        *   `/publicDisplaySettings/theme`: This is a separate, singleton document that *only* contains the `activeThemeName` field. This field is denormalized (copied) from `/storeSettings/mainSettings`. Its security posture is publicly readable by all users.
    *   **Benefit:** This denormalization is critical for Authorization Independence. Public users can `get('/publicDisplaySettings/theme')` without requiring any `get()` calls in security rules to check parent permissions or user roles, and without exposing any sensitive admin data. It makes the public theme access highly performant and the security rule trivially simple (`allow read: true;`). The administrative updates to `activeThemeName` in `/storeSettings/mainSettings` would trigger a Cloud Function to update `/publicDisplaySettings/theme`, ensuring consistency.

3.  **DBAC for Admin Roles:**
    *   Administrator identification is handled by the `/admin_roles/{adminUid}` collection. The mere existence of a document at a specific `adminUid` path grants administrative privileges. This avoids custom claims and simplifies security rules to `exists(/admin_roles/$(request.auth.uid))` for checking admin status.

This structure ensures a secure, scalable, and debuggable Firestore implementation by separating data based on its security requirements, denormalizing public data for efficient access, and using explicit role-based access control.
